author: mark
text:
Make post-delivery machinery follow per-task target_branch

bl-d4b0 let bl review land a task's squash on the task's own
target_branch; bl sync and half-push still assumed one repo-level
integration branch, stranding and falsely flagging per-task
deliveries. bl review now records the effective branch as a
target=<branch> marker on the state review subject (omitted when it
equals the repo default — byte-identical for untouched repos, old
clients ignore the trailing token). bl sync pushes the distinct
recorded targets not yet on the code remote (best-effort, mirroring
the repo-level main push); half-push scans each closed task's
recorded branch with the repo default as fallback. Also closes the
latent config-target-change mis-scan.
